The Porsche reaches 27.78 m/s (62 mph) in 2.7 s.  
Assuming uniform acceleration during that interval:  
a ≈ 27.78 m/s ÷ 2.7 s ≈ 10.3 m/s².  
Distance covered in that 2.7 s: d = 0.5 a t² ≈ 0.5 × 10.3 × (2.7)² ≈ 38 m.  

Distance left to 100 m: 100 m – 38 m = 62 m.  
Even if the car stopped accelerating and just held 27.78 m/s, time for the remaining 62 m = 62 m ÷ 27.78 m/s ≈ 2.2 s.  

Total time ≈ 2.7 s + 2.2 s ≈ 4.9 s, well below Usain Bolt’s 9.58 s 100 m record.  
Therefore the Porsche would finish first.
